_meta.description: Batch 2 state-save patch -- grammar fixes, ANTLR review, dialect gating

M300.title: Close remaining COBOL-85 grammar gaps (26 verified remaining)
M300.description: Grammar audit verified 2026-03-30. Original 138 gaps, ~102 fixed at session start. Batch 1 closed 7 REJECT gaps (29 remaining). Batch 2 closed 3 REJECT gaps (26 remaining). ANTLR review completed: 14 cobolWord propagation fixes, 4 dialect gates, dialect wiring. Remaining REJECT gaps: M407 (CURRENCY SIGN WITH PICTURE SYMBOL), M411 (SCREEN SECTION). 15 OVERLENIENT gaps (P3). 9 SEMANTIC-ONLY gaps (M302/M304/M310).

architectural_changes.cobolWord_rule: Centralized IDENTIFIER + LENGTH/NATIONAL/NORMAL; propagated to dataReference, qualification, programName, procedureName, fileName, computerName, dataName + 17 secondary positions
architectural_changes.lparen_trigger_expansion: Added LENGTH, NATIONAL, NORMAL to subscript-mode trigger list
architectural_changes.dialect_gating: {is2002()}? predicates on STOP STATUS, START WITH LENGTH, FOR ALPHANUMERIC/NATIONAL on CLASS/ALPHABET/SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS
architectural_changes.dialect_wiring: DialectLevel made public on CobolParserCoreBase; Options.Dialect wired through Compilation.LexAndParse to parser.DialectLevel; EndToEndTestBase.CompileAndRun gained dialect parameter

M400.description: SET switch-setting ON/OFF (8 SET formats). Grammar setSwitchStatement was already implemented in prior session. NC174A passes 100% clean (77 PASS, 0 FAIL). No grammar changes needed in Batch 2.
M400.evidence: Verified: setSwitchStatement rule in CobolParserCore.g4 handles SET mnemonic-name+ TO (ON|OFF) compound form. Binder in BoundTreeBuilder.cs routes to setSwitchStatement. NC174A regression: 77 PASS, 0 FAIL. Integration test: Set_SwitchOnOff_StillWorks (GrammarBatch2Tests.cs).
M400.deliverables_status: complete -- no changes needed, already implemented

M402.description: SORT Format 2 (table sort). Grammar: made USING/GIVING/PROCEDURE block optional in sortStatement (CobolIO.g4). Made sortKeyPhrase dataReferenceList optional. Binder: null-check in BindSortKeys. Runtime table sort deferred to M427.
M402.evidence: Grammar delta: sortStatement input/output block wrapped in ()? (CobolIO.g4 line 312-316). sortKeyPhrase: dataReferenceList? (line 326). Binder: BindSortKeys null-check for optional dataReferenceList (FileIoBinder.cs line 530). ANTLR review: confirmed no ambiguity (C2 evaluated CLEAN). Integration tests: Sort_Format2_TableSort, Sort_Format2_DescendingKey, Sort_Format2_WithDuplicates, Sort_Format1_FileSort_StillWorks (GrammarBatch2Tests.cs). Regression: 922 unit + 311 integration + 95 NIST = 0 failures. Sub-gap: M427 (runtime table sort not implemented).
M402.deliverables_status: grammar complete; runtime deferred to M427

M408.description: SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS N:N mapping. Grammar: symbolicCharacterEntry changed from IDENTIFIER (IS|ARE) literal to cobolWord+ (IS|ARE) integerLiteral+ for N:N positional mapping. Added FOR (ALPHANUMERIC|NATIONAL) phrase with {is2002()}? gate. IN clause changed to cobolWord. SemanticBuilder updated for parallel name/ordinal array iteration.
M408.evidence: Grammar delta: symbolicCharacterEntry rule rewritten (CobolSpecialNames.g4 lines 78-79). FOR phrase with dialect gate (line 74). IN cobolWord (line 75). SemanticBuilder: parallel iteration of cobolWord[] and integerLiteral[] arrays (SemanticBuilder.cs lines 323-336). ANTLR review: C1 downgraded -- grammar is unambiguous for well-formed input; N:M count mismatch deferred to semantic validation (M428). Integration tests: SymbolicCharacters_SingleMapping, SymbolicCharacters_NNMapping, SymbolicCharacters_WithInAlphabet, SymbolicCharacters_ForAlphanumeric (GrammarBatch2Tests.cs). Regression: 0 failures. Sub-gap: M428 (count validation).
M408.deliverables_status: grammar + semantic builder complete; count validation deferred to M428

new_items.title: SORT Format 2 runtime -- table sort binder/lowerer/emitter
new_items.description: SORT Format 2 (table sort) grammar is accepted (M402 complete), but the runtime binder resolves the sort target as a FileSymbol. When the target is a data item with OCCURS, the sort is silently skipped. Need: binder detects table sort target (OCCURS clause), lowerer generates in-memory sort IR, emitter produces Array.Sort or equivalent CIL.
deliverables: Detect table sort target in BindSort (OCCURS clause on target data item)
Add BoundTableSortStatement or extend BoundSortStatement with IsTableSort flag
Lower table sort to IR (in-memory sort using key comparisons)
Emit CIL for in-memory table sort (custom comparer for multi-key ascending/descending)
Add integration tests verifying actual sort output correctness
risks: SORT Format 2 appears to work but silently does nothing
Multi-key sort with mixed ascending/descending requires custom comparer
OCCURS DEPENDING ON tables have variable active element count
test_impact: Upgrade GrammarBatch2Tests sort tests from parser-only to output-verifying
New integration tests for multi-key table sort
New integration tests for SORT with OCCURS DEPENDING ON

new_items.title: Semantic validation: SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS N:N count equality
new_items.description: Grammar accepts mismatched N:M counts in SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS entries (e.g., 3 names mapped to 2 integers). COBOL-85 spec section 12.3.7 rule 16c requires one-to-one correspondence between symbolic-character-1 occurrences and integer-1 occurrences. SemanticBuilder should emit a diagnostic when name count != integer count.
deliverables: Add count validation in SemanticBuilder symbolic character processing
Emit diagnostic (e.g., COBOL0xxx) when name count != integer count
Add negative parser test for mismatched counts
risks: Incorrect collating sequence definitions accepted silently
Programs with N:M mismatch produce wrong character mappings at runtime
test_impact: Diagnostic unit tests for count mismatch
Negative integration test for SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS with mismatched counts
